bugs.error_message: ThrowOnError still shows notification banner before re-throwing
bugs.root_cause: LogError fired ErrorLogged event (notification banner) before the re-throw check. In debug mode, error surfaces both as a banner AND as a thrown exception, creating confusing noise alongside the debugger break.
bugs.fix: Moved ThrowOnError re-throw to execute BEFORE ErrorLogged.Invoke(). When ThrowOnError is true the method exits via Throw() so the banner event is never fired.

bugs.error_message: ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION: process cannot access file because it is used by another process
bugs.root_cause: File.Copy and File.OpenRead use FileShare.Read (FILE_SHARE_READ). DatabaseContext holds D open with GENERIC_READ|GENERIC_WRITE. Windows requires new opener FileShare to cover ALL existing handles access rights; FileShare.Read does not cover GENERIC_WRITE.
bugs.fix: Added CopyWithSharing helper (FileShare.ReadWrite on source). Changed ComputeHash to use FileStream with FileShare.ReadWrite. Replaced all 5 File.Copy calls with CopyWithSharing.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Menu bar shows working copy name (e.g. A90CDE58_TU5-TT) instead of source DB name (TU5-TT); Save fails with access denied on startup from most-recent database
bugs.root_cause: RunReturningUserStartupAsync passed dbPath (D', the working copy) to SetupMainWindowAsync after RunCheckoutAsync overwrote it with the working-copy path. SetDatabaseName was never called with SourcePath (D), so the menu bar showed the GUID-prefixed working copy filename. SaveAsync then tried to finalize the save by moving D.tmp to D, but D was the wrong path (D') which was already open by SQLite, causing ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION.
bugs.fix: Added canonicalPath resolution (App.Checkout.SourcePath in write mode, else dbPath) before InitializeServices. Called vm.SetDatabaseName(canonicalPath, canonicalPath) explicitly before SetupMainWindowAsync, and passed canonicalPath to SetupMainWindowAsync so the backup session also targets D. Mirrors the existing fix in SwitchDatabaseAsync.

bugs.error_message: App freezes then throws 'SQLite Error: no such table: Sections' when clicking 'Switch to edit mode' from read-only mode
bugs.root_cause: Double-checkout in AcquireWriteLock: called CheckoutAsync(D) directly (acquiring lock, creating D'), then passed WorkingPath (D') to SwitchDatabaseAsync instead of D. SwitchDatabaseAsync is designed for D: it released the lock, deleted D', then re-ran RunCheckoutAsync(D') on the now-missing path. CheckoutAsync took the new-database shortcut (file doesn't exist), creating an empty DB at D'. The synchronous BackupSqliteDatabase call in SaveAsync also blocked the UI thread, causing the visible freeze.
bugs.fix: Removed pre-checkout from AcquireWriteLock. Now calls SwitchDatabaseAsync(sourcePath) directly with D, delegating the full release→checkout→reinit cycle to SwitchDatabaseAsync/RunCheckoutAsync as intended.

bugs.error_message: System.ArgumentException: Control does not belong to visual tree (ListBoxItem.OnPointerReleased → ItemSelectionEventTriggers.IsPointerEventWithinBounds → PointToScreen)
bugs.root_cause: Two Avalonia bugs intersect: (1) PlatformImpl goes null on native popup windows (regression since 11.3.7, #19892), and (2) Avalonia 12 added IsPointerEventWithinBounds which calls PointToScreen during PointerReleased on ListBoxItem. When the popup's native window backing is already destroyed, PointToScreen throws. Triggered by AutoCompleteBox dropdowns in complex DataTemplate visual trees.
bugs.fix: Set Win32PlatformOptions { OverlayPopups = true } in Program.cs. Overlay popups render within the main window, eliminating native popup windows entirely. See WORKAROUNDS.md entry #1.

bugs.error_message: TourCatalog.ScanResources finds 0 steps/segments/tours — catalog empty at runtime
bugs.root_cause: Avalonia lazily wraps AXAML-defined objects as PointerDeferredContent<T>. Iterating IResourceDictionary yields the deferred wrapper, not the materialized TourStepData/TourSegmentData/TourData. The switch(kvp.Value) never matched any tour types.
bugs.fix: Replaced direct kvp.Value access with resources.TryGetResource(key, null, out var resolved) to force materialization before type-checking.

bugs.error_message: Noticeable UI lag after clicking Apply on a section edit (WASM build); filter re-render stayed brisk by comparison
bugs.root_cause: Not the DB write-back (WASM uses in-memory DemoSectionRepository, no persistence). Two causes: (1) the onSave callback called _sectionStore.Reload AND Load(), but the VM is subscribed to SectionsChanged->Reload, so the section list was rebuilt twice; (2) every refresh assigned a brand-new ObservableCollection to SectionItems, forcing the ListBox into a CollectionChanged.Reset that tears down and re-realizes every card container (~425ms for 63 cards).
bugs.fix: In onSave, unsubscribe this VM's SectionsChanged->Reload handler around _sectionStore.Reload so the list refreshes once (Grid + Workload still notified). For edits of an existing section, added TryUpdateCardInPlace(): build one fresh card VM and do a granular Remove+Insert at the correct sorted position instead of replacing the whole collection, so only one container is re-realized. New/unfound sections fall back to full Load. Apply dropped from ~1094ms to ~730ms; list no longer flash-rebuilds.
